path_corrections.severity: WARN — path error in prompt, not a functional STOP
path_corrections.citation: CONFIRMED: Glob for backend/config/questionnaire.json returned no results. Glob for **/questionnaire.json returned backend/ssot/questionnaire.json and frontend/app/lib/mock/questionnaire.json. Authoritative backend questionnaire is backend/ssot/questionnaire.json — consistent with CLAUDE.md and MEMORY.md SSOT structure documentation.
path_corrections.required_action: Cursor must use backend/ssot/questionnaire.json in all authority preflight and inspection steps where the prompt references backend/config/questionnaire.json. This does not block execution but must be corrected in Cursor's working context.

stage_2a_authority_verification.duplicate_authority_sources: NONE detected — single questionnaire at backend/ssot/questionnaire.json is authoritative.

intelligence_core_components_affected: backend/core/analytics/runtime_context_evaluator.py — build_runtime_context_snapshot() may require narrow deterministic additions for missing optional context keys
stage_2b_behavioural_hardening.behavioural_surface_area: Pregnancy gate changes will allow FAI high, FT3 low, and free testosterone high signals to fire for users where pregnancy_status is not_answered (i.e. all real users today, since questionnaire has no pregnancy_status field). This is a user-visible output change — these signals will emit where they are currently suppressed for all users. This is the intended correction.
regression_targets: DHEA-S high remains active (pkg_kb47_dhea_high_androgen_excess_context pregnancy gate already allows not_answered — signal_library.yaml:69-73)
Batch 2 active package count remains 5 (no new activation, no deactivation)
pregnancy answered_yes remains suppressive for all three carry-forward packages

Whether Batch 2 active package count is exactly 5 — Cursor must confirm via governance validators at Phase 7
Whether day-one launch estate gate passes before modification — Cursor must run validate_day_one_launch_estate_gate.py in authority preflight

fai_high.required_fix: Add not_answered and not_applicable to allowed_values per sprint Phase 1 preferred outcome A

ft3_low.runtime_state: pregnancy_status = not_answered for all real users → gate fails → signal SUPPRESSED for all real users.

free_testosterone_high.runtime_state: pregnancy_status = not_answered for all real users → gate fails → signal SUPPRESSED for all real users.

dhea_s_high.runtime_state: pregnancy_status = not_answered for all real users → not_answered IS in allowed_values → gate PASSES → DHEA-S high is reachable. This is the correctly-gated reference model for the other three packages.

additional_unreachability_findings.severity: HIGH — blocks multiple signals for all real users
additional_unreachability_findings.title: symptoms_status gate: key never produced by runtime_context_evaluator for real questionnaire responses
additional_unreachability_findings.evaluator_citation: CONFIRMED: runtime_context_evaluator.py:415 — _field_answered(responses, 'symptoms') — only sets symptoms_status if the key 'symptoms' exists in questionnaire_responses
additional_unreachability_findings.questionnaire_citation: CONFIRMED: backend/ssot/questionnaire.json full content read — no question with id: 'symptoms'. Has 'regular_migraines' (line 189), 'recent_infections' (line 204), 'low_testosterone_symptoms' (line 240) — none map to the key 'symptoms'
additional_unreachability_findings.runtime_consequence: symptoms_status is NEVER set in clinical_context bucket for any real user. gate evaluator: observed = bucket.get('symptoms_status') = None. None not in {answered_yes, answered_no} → gate fails → signal suppressed for all users.
additional_unreachability_findings.phase_0_instruction: Cursor MUST document this in Phase 0 audit table. Sprint Phase 0 explicitly requires auditing 'whether the key is produced by runtime_context_evaluator' and 'whether the key is actually captured by questionnaire.json'. This is exactly the class of issue the sprint is designed to detect.
additional_unreachability_findings.phase_4_instruction: Runtime context evaluator review (Phase 4) must determine whether symptoms_status can be derived from existing questionnaire fields (regular_migraines, recent_infections, symptoms_related fields) via a narrow deterministic mapping, OR whether a new questionnaire field would be required. If a new field is required, STOP condition 7 applies. If deterministic mapping from existing fields is possible, this is an allowed Phase 4 small deterministic addition.
additional_unreachability_findings.stop_condition_risk: STOP 7 (new questionnaire field required) and STOP 11 (broad context redesign) may apply. Cursor must assess and escalate if triggered.

additional_unreachability_findings.severity: HIGH — blocks signals for users who take ANY supplement
additional_unreachability_findings.title: aas_exposure_status evaluator logic: inherits supplement_state wholesale, suppressing users with any supplement
additional_unreachability_findings.evaluator_citation: CONFIRMED: runtime_context_evaluator.py:390 — aas_state = supplement_state if supplements_answered else DISCLOSURE_NOT_ANSWERED. supplement_state is DISCLOSURE_ANSWERED_YES for any supplement list containing values other than None/no/not_applicable (e.g. ['Multivitamin', 'Vitamin D']). aas_state = DISCLOSURE_ANSWERED_YES before steroid/prohormone check (lines 404-405). aas_exposure_status is set to answered_yes for any user who takes any supplement at all.
additional_unreachability_findings.questionnaire_citation: CONFIRMED: backend/ssot/questionnaire.json:297-310 — 'supplements' field with options: Multivitamin, Vitamin D, Omega-3/Fish Oil, Probiotics, Iron, Other, None. required: false.
additional_unreachability_findings.runtime_consequence: User selects [Multivitamin, Vitamin D] → supplements_answered=True → supplement_state=answered_yes → aas_state=answered_yes (before steroid check, no steroid found, stays answered_yes) → aas_exposure_status=answered_yes → gate requires answered_no → FAIL. Signal suppressed for a user who takes only vitamins.
additional_unreachability_findings.phase_0_instruction: Cursor MUST document in Phase 0 audit. This is an evaluator logic bug within Phase 4 scope.
additional_unreachability_findings.phase_4_instruction: aas_exposure_status should default to answered_no when supplements are answered but contain no AAS/steroid/prohormone keywords, not inherit supplement_state wholesale. A narrow deterministic fix is: aas_state = DISCLOSURE_ANSWERED_NO if supplements_answered and no AAS keywords found; DISCLOSURE_ANSWERED_YES only when AAS keywords detected. This is a small deterministic addition permitted by Phase 4 scope.
additional_unreachability_findings.stop_condition_risk: STOP 11 (broad context model redesign) does NOT apply — this is a narrow evaluator logic bug. However, Cursor must confirm the fix does not affect other signals before making the change.

additional_unreachability_findings.severity: MEDIUM — affects FT3 low and free testosterone low for users who do not provide lifestyle factors
additional_unreachability_findings.title: calorie_restriction_status and fasting_status: depend on lifestyle_factors upstream mapping, not questionnaire fields directly
additional_unreachability_findings.evaluator_citation: CONFIRMED: runtime_context_evaluator.py:432-445 — maps lifestyle_factors['calorie_restriction'] → calorie_restriction_status and lifestyle_factors['fasting'] → fasting_status. These are only set if the corresponding key exists in lifestyle_factors.
additional_unreachability_findings.questionnaire_citation: CONFIRMED: backend/ssot/questionnaire.json — has 'fasting_hours' (line 265, id: fasting_hours) and 'dietary_pattern' (line 312) but no key named 'calorie_restriction' or 'fasting'. Whether lifestyle_factors['fasting'] is assembled from questionnaire responses['fasting_hours'] cannot be verified from the evaluator alone — requires checking upstream pipeline assembly.
additional_unreachability_findings.phase_0_instruction: Cursor must verify in Phase 0 audit whether the upstream pipeline maps questionnaire 'fasting_hours' to lifestyle_factors['fasting'] and 'dietary_pattern' to lifestyle_factors['calorie_restriction']. If no mapping exists, these gates will NEVER be satisfied → signals always suppressed. If mapping exists, confirm it is deterministic.
additional_unreachability_findings.stop_condition_risk: STOP 7 may apply if no upstream mapping exists and these fields require new questionnaire questions.

stop_condition_pre_assessment.stop_5_pregnancy_semantics_cannot_be_res: NOT TRIGGERED — DHEA-S high reference model provides clear precedent: not_answered and not_applicable safe; answered_yes suppressive

stop_condition_pre_assessment.stop_7_new_questionnaire_field_required: RISK for symptoms_status (AUF-1) and potentially calorie_restriction/fasting (AUF-3). Cursor must assess in Phase 0/4 and STOP if new questions are required.
stop_condition_pre_assessment.stop_8_through_19: NOT triggered by prompt structure — appropriate non-scope exclusions in place

implementation_warnings.title: Path error in prompt: questionnaire referenced at wrong path
implementation_warnings.citation: Prompt references backend/config/questionnaire.json — file does NOT exist. Actual authoritative path: backend/ssot/questionnaire.json (CONFIRMED by Glob).
implementation_warnings.required_action: MANDATORY: Cursor must use backend/ssot/questionnaire.json in all Phase 0 audit steps and Phase 4 review. Do not attempt to read backend/config/questionnaire.json — it does not exist.

implementation_warnings.title: Pregnancy gate: all three carry-forward packages suppress all real users today
implementation_warnings.citation: FAI high signal_library.yaml:92-94; FT3 low signal_library.yaml:87-89; Free testosterone high signal_library.yaml:92-94 — all require pregnancy_status: [answered_no] only. runtime_context_evaluator.py:298-302 — sets not_answered when no pregnancy_status field in responses. backend/ssot/questionnaire.json — no pregnancy_status field confirmed by full content read.
implementation_warnings.required_action: MANDATORY Phase 1: add not_answered and not_applicable to pregnancy_status allowed_values for FAI high and free testosterone high per preferred outcome A. For FT3 low: clinically assess whether pregnancy-specific suppression is required before applying outcome A. answered_yes must remain suppressive for all three.

implementation_warnings.title: symptoms_status gate: never produced for real users — compounding suppression for FAI high, free testosterone high, free testosterone low
implementation_warnings.citation: runtime_context_evaluator.py:415; backend/ssot/questionnaire.json full content — no 'symptoms' id. Affects signal_library.yaml lines 86-89 (FAI high), 87-89 (FT3 high), 74-78 (FT3 low).
implementation_warnings.required_action: MANDATORY Phase 0 audit capture, Phase 4 assessment. Cursor must determine whether a narrow deterministic mapping from existing questionnaire fields to symptoms_status is possible. If not, STOP condition 7 applies.

implementation_warnings.title: aas_exposure_status evaluator bug: supplements answered_yes (any supplement) incorrectly sets aas_exposure_status to answered_yes
implementation_warnings.citation: runtime_context_evaluator.py:390 — aas_state = supplement_state if supplements_answered. CONFIRMED: any non-empty/non-none supplement list produces supplement_state = DISCLOSURE_ANSWERED_YES. Gate requires answered_no for FAI high (signal_library.yaml:77-79), free testosterone high (lines 75-77), free testosterone low (lines 97-100).
implementation_warnings.required_action: MANDATORY Phase 4 assessment. Narrow deterministic fix: aas_state should = DISCLOSURE_ANSWERED_NO when supplements are answered but no AAS/steroid/prohormone keywords detected; only DISCLOSURE_ANSWERED_YES when AAS keywords found. Cursor must confirm scope of fix does not exceed Phase 4 allowed change.

implementation_warnings.title: calorie_restriction_status and fasting_status reachability depends on unverified upstream pipeline mapping
implementation_warnings.citation: runtime_context_evaluator.py:432-445; backend/ssot/questionnaire.json has fasting_hours and dietary_pattern but not fasting or calorie_restriction keys. Affects FT3 low and free testosterone low.
implementation_warnings.required_action: Phase 0 audit: verify upstream pipeline mapping of questionnaire fields to lifestyle_factors. If no mapping, assess STOP condition 7.

implementation_warnings.title: free_testosterone_low is not one of the named carry-forward packages but also has gate issues
implementation_warnings.citation: pkg_kb47_free_testosterone_low_androgen_deficiency_context/signal_library.yaml:74-78 (symptoms_status), lines 85-90 (calorie_restriction_status), lines 97-100 (aas_exposure_status). This package was not listed in the named three carry-forward packages but has the same class of issues.
implementation_warnings.required_action: Phase 0 audit must include free testosterone low as specified in the prompt audit list (line 9 of Phase 0 section). Decisions for symptoms_status and aas_exposure_status fixes should cover this package consistently.

hardening_notes: Prompt is structurally sound and correctly classified HIGH/BEHAVIOUR/TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH. All five front matter fields are valid. One path correction required (questionnaire at backend/ssot/ not backend/config/). Carry-forward issue fully verified from source content: all three named packages have pregnancy_status gates allowing only answered_no, while the questionnaire has no pregnancy_status field and the evaluator produces not_answered by default — signals are confirmed suppressed for all real users today. DHEA-S high is confirmed correctly gated (allows not_answered) and is the reference model. Two additional unreachability findings surface beyond the stated carry-forward: (AUF-1) symptoms_status never produced for real users — affects FAI high, free testosterone high, and free testosterone low; (AUF-2) aas_exposure_status evaluator logic incorrectly inherits supplement_state, suppressing signals for any supplement user. These are within the sprint's explicit Phase 0 audit scope and Phase 4 evaluator review scope. STOP conditions 7 and 11 must be assessed during implementation for symptoms_status. All STOP conditions are appropriate. GPT architectural review and human approval required before merge per HIGH risk route.
